title: Add M4 obligations and town-rule slice
summary: Add focused obligation and executable town-rule owner state for lamp oil duty, lodging witness duty, name confirmation and night-knock compliance.
acceptance: Obligation state owns creditor debtor type condition due window visibility inheritance basis fulfillment action violation consequence source event and current state
TownRule state owns subject scope time region trigger action exception enforcement method cost legitimacy source penalty and state
Lampkeeper oil duty lodging witness duty name confirmation and night-knock compliance are represented as explicit facts with structured reasons
Compliance reads knowledge needs relationship fear enforcement risk emergency and obligation facts through bounded candidates rather than global scans
Focused tests cover fulfillment violation due-index cleanup compliance exceptions enforcement costs structured reasons and no hidden mechanical text

history.details: Created by WM-0061 M4 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Implement the minimal obligation and town-rule owner surfaces needed for the M4 borrowed-shadow lamp scenario.
forbiddenPaths: broad governance faction campaign or M5 content framework
public save format or schema changes
hidden narrative flag or text-only rule workaround
benchmarkImpact: Adds due-obligation compliance candidate violation fulfillment and rule-cost counters; baseline updates wait for WM-0070.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; systems-architect must be consulted before changing obligation ownership, town-rule semantics or save scope.
rollbackModel: Revert obligation/town-rule stores, tests, metrics and docs; preserve prior lamp and Chronicle owner surfaces.
ownerGate: Keep obligations and town rules as owner facts
Do not hide mechanical conditions in text
Use bounded due and compliance indexes
Block on public protocol save schema dependency UI authority broad governance M5 or director scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Obligation and executable town-rule ownership affects deterministic simulation and cannot be assigned to Spark.
